author: Dr. Andre Williams
content:
PRIMARY CARE VISIT — 9/15/2026, 2:45 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Visit type: Established patient, urgent add-on
Interpreter: In-person Spanish-speaking MA (Ms. Lopez)

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Worsening joint pain, fatigue, weight loss, and intermittent rash since HCQ ran out 5 weeks ago.

HPI:
Ms. Sandoval is a 23-year-old woman (DOB 8/12/2003, recently turned 23) with undifferentiated connective tissue disease / possible early SLE, Hashimoto's thyroiditis on levothyroxine, who returns for re-evaluation. Last visit with me was 4/24/2026; in the interval she had her rheumatology consultation (4/10/2026) and was started on prednisone taper (completed 6/1/2026) plus continuation of HCQ. She has been out of HCQ since approximately 8/12/2026 due to the manufacturer PAP renewal denial documented in our pharmacy/care-coordination note 8/22/2026.

Since running out of HCQ:
- Joint pain markedly worse: Bilateral wrist, MCP, and PIP pain with morning stiffness now >2 hours, sometimes lasting until early afternoon. Now also reports knee pain that was not present before. Difficulty with her work — has reduced shifts to 12-15 hours/week (from 25-30 hours/week at baseline).
- Fatigue: 'Mucho cansancio' — she reports she 'sleeps and is still tired.' She has missed several work shifts.
- Rash: New, intermittent, pinkish rash on chest, upper back, and now arms — described by patient as 'aparece y desaparece' (appears and disappears). Worse after sun exposure. No oral ulcers. No painful or pruritic component — primarily concerning to her cosmetically and as a 'new thing.'
- Hair: Continued diffuse shedding. She has noticed thinning is now more visible at the crown.
- Weight: She does not own a scale. She reports clothing is loose. Notes she has been 'not as hungry' and skipping meals more frequently to save money.
- New: Some occasional difficulty with chewing tougher foods — she attributes this to jaw tightness when she chews steak or beef stew. Denies overt dysphagia, denies food sticking in her throat, denies regurgitation. (I probed this carefully — see below.)
- Denies: fever, chest pain, dyspnea, dark or foamy urine, leg swelling, eye pain or vision change, focal weakness, headache.
- Menses: now skipped for 2 months (no prior pregnancy; not sexually active).
- Mood: She acknowledges feeling 'triste' (sad). PHQ-2 score 3 in clinic today; PHQ-9 administered = 11 (moderate depression). No SI/HI.

MEDS (per pharmacy verification today):
- Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID — last filled 5/12/2026, supply ended ~8/12/2026, not currently taking.
- Levothyroxine 25 mcg PO daily — continued, last filled 8/30/2026.
- Ibuprofen 400-600 mg PRN — using daily for joint pain, sometimes 2-3 doses/day; counseled re: NSAID risks.
- Multivitamin — intermittent.

VITALS:
BP 102/64, HR 96, RR 16, T 98.0 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 46.4 kg (down from 50.8 in January 2026 — total loss ~4.4 kg / ~8.7% over 8 months). Height 150 cm. BMI 20.6. Pain 6/10 (joints).

FOCUSED EXAM:
General: Thin young woman, appears tired, mildly cachetic. Mother present and visibly worried. Engaged via interpreter.
Skin: Faint, blanching, blotchy pink rash on V-area of chest and across upper back/shoulders. Sparing flexures. Sparing nasolabial folds. No malar distribution today. No discoid lesions. No ulcers. Diffuse scalp hair thinning more apparent than at last visit; visible scalp at crown.
HEENT: Oropharynx clear, no ulcers (specifically inspected). No thrush. No cervical lymphadenopathy. Thyroid not enlarged.
CV: RRR, no murmurs, no rub. Capillary refill brisk.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly.
MSK: Symmetric synovitis of MCPs, PIPs, and wrists worse than prior — increased tenderness, mild dorsal swelling. Now also mild left knee effusion with restricted full flexion. Ankles tender. No deformities. Grip strength 3/5 bilaterally (decreased from prior). Proximal muscle strength: shoulder abduction 4+/5, hip flexion 4+/5, knee extension 5/5 — possibly subtly reduced from prior baseline but patient resistance is limited by joint pain; difficult to fully assess for proximal weakness in setting of pain.
Swallow screen (bedside): I asked her to take a sip of water and observe; no overt coughing or choking. Asked her to chew a small piece of bread and swallow — accomplished without difficulty. Voice normal. No drooling. No nasal regurgitation reported.
Neuro: Cranial nerves grossly intact. Sensation intact. DTRs 2+ throughout. No focal deficits.

LABS DRAWN TODAY (sliding-scale lab):
- CBC: WBC 3.85 (mildly low; ref 4.0-11.0), Hgb 11.1 (low; ref 12.0-15.5), MCV 84 (normal), Plt 218 (normal)
- CMP: Na 137, K 3.9, Cl 102, CO2 23, BUN 8, Cr 0.5, Glu 86, Ca 8.7 (low-normal; ref 8.5-10.2), Albumin 3.4 (low; ref 3.5-5.0), Total protein 6.6 (low-normal), AST 28, ALT 22, ALP 78, Total bilirubin 0.5
- TSH 4.21 (mildly elevated; she may have missed doses)
- ESR 38 (elevated; ref <20)
- CRP 1.8 mg/dL (elevated; ref <0.5)
- UA: trace protein, no blood, no leukocytes; UPC ratio pending
- Anti-dsDNA, complement C3/C4 — sent send-out
- Ferritin, B12, folate, vitamin D 25-OH — sent send-out
- Pregnancy test (urine HCG): negative

IMAGING:
No new imaging today. Hand X-rays from rheumatology (4/10/2026) — I do not have results in this chart but will request from Hopkins.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

23-year-old woman with undifferentiated CTD / possible early SLE off HCQ for ~5 weeks, with significant clinical worsening:
- Active inflammatory polyarthritis (worsened, now including knee involvement)
- Constitutional symptoms with 4.4 kg weight loss (~8.7% body weight)
- New photosensitive-distribution rash on V-area of chest and upper back
- Mild cytopenias (Hgb 11.1, WBC 3.85) — new, concerning
- Mildly elevated inflammatory markers (ESR 38, CRP 1.8)
- Borderline hypoalbuminemia (3.4)
- Moderate depression (PHQ-9 11)
- Subjective jaw / chewing fatigue — patient does not describe overt dysphagia; bedside swallow screen unremarkable. Will monitor closely.

This represents an evolving phenotype concerning for an active connective tissue disease flare driven by medication interruption. The new cytopenias, falling albumin, weight loss, and photosensitive rash raise concern for SLE meeting more classification criteria, with possible early systemic involvement that has not yet been characterized.

PLAN:
1. URGENT HCQ bridge: I am writing for a 30-day cash-pay supply at $32 from a discount pharmacy I have used for other uninsured patients (low-cost source identified by Carolina). Mother will pick up today. Counseled to take 200 mg BID immediately upon obtaining.
2. Re-engage rheumatology URGENTLY: I have personally messaged Dr. Tanaka at Hopkins through Care Everywhere portal, attaching today's labs and clinical summary, requesting urgent evaluation. I have also placed a referral note to the Casa Ruben Free Clinic which has a volunteer rheumatologist on Thursday evenings.
3. Hold ibuprofen given mild low albumin, mild creatinine, and concern for renal involvement; tylenol 1g q6h PRN as bridge.
4. Address depression: PHQ-9 11, declined pharmacotherapy today, accepted referral to Esperanza Behavioral Health (Spanish-speaking therapist, sliding-scale).
5. Nutrition: Patient referred to community food bank (already enrolled per Ms. Mercado). I have asked her to keep a brief food diary for 1 week. Spanish-language nutrition handouts provided.
6. Repeat labs ordered today (CBC, CMP, ESR, CRP, UPC, anti-dsDNA, C3/C4, ferritin, B12, folate, 25-OH vit D). Will follow up by phone when send-outs return (~7-10 days).
7. Return precautions reviewed in Spanish: new fever, chest pain, dyspnea, severe headache, focal weakness, dark/bloody urine, foamy urine, leg/face swelling, vision change, oral ulcers — go to ED. Also: if joint pain markedly worsens or new symptom emerges, call us.
8. Follow-up: 2-3 weeks with me in clinic (target 10/8/2026); pending rheumatology response in interim.
9. Diana Mercado, LCSW, was paged and met patient and mother in clinic at the end of this visit to follow up on Medicaid appeal status.

Visit time: 60 minutes (interpreter time 35 min). Notes shared with patient via clinic patient portal in Spanish.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Andre Williams, MD
Esperanza Health (FQHC)
Date: 9/15/2026
